works: 2-3 word labels dominate POD — they are badges the buyer applies to themselves

example: "Diagnosed With Main Character Syndrome", "Diagnosed With Chronic Tiredness"
works: Fake diagnosis humor is a top Redbubble pattern — signals membership in a group

example: "I'm Not Lazy, I'm Energy Efficient", "I'm Not Late, I'm On My Own Schedule"
works: Reframes a flaw as a flex — buyers identify instantly

example: "Panic First, Function Later", "Nap First, Adult Later"
works: Rule-of-two structure with a clear priority — easy to read, easy to wear

example: "It's Not Loitering, It's Loafing", "It's Not Laziness, It's Energy Conservation"
works: Bestseller pattern — rebrands a negative trait as intentional

example: "Pigeoning: The Art of Showing Up Uninvited", "Goosing: The Art of Chaos"
works: Pseudo-academic framing elevates low-stakes behavior — high shareability

example: "Eat. Sleep. Regret.", "Show Up. Space Out. Leave."
works: Most shared meme format — the third beat delivers the punchline
Mirror of meme templates — feels familiar, lands fast
Earnest delusion reads as ironic — buyer pretends to be confident

example: "I'm Literally the Best", "I'm Literally Him", "I'm Literally That Girl"
works: Declarative self-praise with no irony gap — absurd confidence is the joke
Credential format applied to uncredentialable traits — instant meme recognition

example: "You Did This To Me", "The Government Did This", "My Last Brain Cell Did This"
works: Assigns agency where none exists — paranoid humor

example: "I Told You So", "I Told You This Would Happen"
works: Vindication humor — smugness as a punchline
Maximum impact in minimum words — pure reaction, no setup
Question as statement — the confusion IS the joke

example: "Remember When", "Miss the Old Me", "Take Me Back"
works: Longing framed as confession — vulnerable but relatable
Threat framed as memory — dark but playful
Minimalist mischief — the word carries the tone
Apathy as punchline — the most low-effort humor possible
